Disclaimer: Call 911 in case of emergency; 988 for suicide prevention lifeline; This is my first-person account of my own journey; This is not advice. Full acceptable use policy.
← Back to Recovery Life Skills

My Sobriety Journey: Weeks 9–22

Weeks 9–22
First 90 Days Home: 80 Meetings in 90 Days [Steps 4–8]
I decided that I would spend 2 hours a day on recovery activities.
Returned home and immediately committed to intensive meeting attendance.
Attended 80 AA meetings within the first 90 days back.
Worked Steps 4 through 8 with my sponsor.
Held a group-conscience position at my Primary Purpose home group — “The Speaker Guy.”
I got cravings when too much stress
I got cravings whenever I was bored
I do not go to restaurants with a bar
I switched from 87 and 88 to Bible.
Had to avoid places and things that were triggers. For me, one example is the neighborhood pool.
Attended monthly business meetings and voted on group decisions.
My sponsees began calling me for the Upon Awakening process, so I started adding much more variety into my program to meet their needs
Continued the Upon Awakening process with phone calls and meditations, but now switched to the Bible.
Continued sponsoring other men [Steps 10–12] while suffering from severe mental illness
Alcohol is banned from my house forever.
  • Attending AA meetings
    • Discussion
    • Speaker
    • Big Book
    • Home Group
    • Service Work
    • Took Notes and did research when I got home
  • Audio Sobriety Recordings
    • Speaker Recordings
    • Podcasts
    • Sobriety Videos
  • Therapy
    • Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
    • Talk Therapy
    • Group Therapy
  • Bible Study
    • Podcasts
    • Videos
    • Reading
    • Audio Reading
  • 12 Steps
    • Upon Awakening
    • Handing our flaws over to God
    • Traditional AA sponsorship
  • Meditation
    • Solo Meditation
    • Solo Prayer
    • Group Meditation and Prayer
    • Devotion
  • Church
    • Bible Studies
    • Christian Support Groups
    • Church Services
    • Church Service Projects
    • Volunteer Work
  • Medical
    • Prescriptions
    • Psychiatrist
  • Community
    • Support Groups
    • Community Service Projects
    • Volunteer Work
  • Professional Facilities
    • Detox Center
    • In-Patient Rehab Treatment
    • Out-Patient Rehab Treatment
    • Sober Living
    • Respite
  • Recovery Groups
    • Celebrate Recovery
    • Alcoholics Anonymous
    • SMART Recovery
  • Sponsorship and Peer Support
    • Continue to call and leave message or talk to sponsor every day
    • Started having sponsees calling every day and talk to sponsor when help is needed
    • Peer Support (See CPS-AD)
  • Personal Health
    • Exercise
    • Healthy Food
    • Hydration
    • Consistent Sleep
    • Healthy Food
    • Hydration
